name: CFSC Currency Exchange Wilson & Broadway Check Cashing and Auto License
description_short: CFSC Currency Exchange offers check cashing, money orders, auto registration, and foreign currency exchange at their Chicago location on Broadway.
description_long:
CFSC: Wilson Broadway Currency Exchange Inc. is a currency exchange and check-cashing service located at 4559 N. Broadway in Chicago, Illinois. The business operates extended hours, opening as early as 6:00 AM on weekdays and staying open until 10:00 PM most evenings, with weekend service from 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM on Saturday and 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on Sunday. This location is part of the Currency Exchange Association network.

The company provides a comprehensive suite of financial and government services beyond basic check cashing. Core offerings include check cashing (personal and business), money orders, money transfers, and foreign currency exchange. They also facilitate government-related transactions such as auto title and registration services, license plate renewals, Cook County vital records certificates, and city vehicle stickers. Additional services include ATM access, electronic bill pay, notary public services, and prepaid products like Visa cards and phone cards. The business has recently expanded into cryptocurrency, offering Bitcoin services.

CFSC distinguishes itself by consolidating multiple financial and administrative services under one roof, eliminating the need for customers to visit separate government offices or institutions. Their extended operating hours cater to working individuals who cannot access traditional bank hours. The company explicitly positions itself as an alternative to corporate banks, emphasizing personal service and customer-centric operations with bilingual (English/Spanish) support.

However, as a check-cashing service rather than a bank, CFSC does not offer traditional deposit accounts, savings products, or credit services. Customers should verify specific document requirements before visiting for auto registration services, as Illinois Secretary of State regulations can be particular. The company's fees for check cashing and money services are not disclosed on their website, which is typical for this industry but prevents transparent rate comparison.
pros: Extended hours: Open 6:00 AM-10:00 PM weekdays, accommodating working customers outside traditional banking hours
Consolidated services: Handles check cashing, auto registration, vital records, and bill pay in one location
Government services integration: Direct connection with Illinois Secretary of State for immediate license plate processing
Bilingual service: Offers English and Spanish support for diverse customer base
Wide service array: Bitcoin, prepaid cards, money transfers, notary, coin counting, and private mailboxes available
One-stop convenience: Eliminates multiple trips to banks, government offices, and utility payment locations
ATM access: On-site ATM for immediate cash withdrawal with balance inquiry capability
cons: No fee transparency: Check cashing and money service fees not listed on website, requiring in-person inquiry
Limited product scope: Does not offer savings accounts, deposit services, or credit products
Document requirements: Auto registration services require specific documents; customers must call ahead to verify needs
Single location focus: Website primarily profiles this one Broadway location without mention of other branches
No online services: All transactions appear to require in-person visits; no digital check deposit or remote services mentioned
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked Chicago residents needing check cashing and money transfer services
Working professionals requiring evening/weekend access to government services like auto registration
Spanish-speaking customers seeking bilingual financial and administrative services
Individuals managing multiple financial tasks who prefer consolidated, one-stop locations
diagnosis: CFSC is best for Chicago residents without traditional bank accounts who need convenient access to check cashing, money transfers, and government services with extended evening hours and bilingual support. Primary caveat: this is a transaction-based service provider, not a financial institution, and does not offer deposit accounts, savings, credit products, or digital banking services.
services: License Plate Renewals and City Vehicle Stickers
